title: Covering indexes avoid the clustered-index lookup
description: When a secondary index contains every column a query needs, InnoDB serves it without a second read of the clustered index.
body: Reference for index design on read-hot queries. Because secondary indexes store the primary key, a query whose selected and filtered columns all live in one secondary index is covered and skips the extra clustered-index lookup per row. Use when a hot SELECT touches few columns but many rows. Do not over-stuff an index with columns just to cover a rare query; each added column enlarges the index and slows writes. Validation signals include EXPLAIN showing Using index in the Extra column and a measured latency drop. Risk boundary: a very wide covering index trades write speed and space for read speed, so confirm the read path justifies it before adding columns.
